answer: the Unix form using ASCII LF (linefeed), the Windows form using the ASCII sequence CR LF (return followed by linefeed), the ‘Classic Mac OS’ form using the ASCII CR (return) character

Two or more physical lines may be joined into logical lines using backslash characters (\), as follows: when a physical line ends in a backslash that is not part of a string literal or comment, it is joined with the following forming a single logical line, deleting the backslash and the following end-of-line character.

Uppercase and lowercase letters (A-Z and a-z), the underscore (_), digits (0 through 9) which cannot appear as the first character, and non-ASCII characters that are “letter-like” and “digit-like” according to the Unicode XID_Start and XID_Continue sets.
the backslash is left in the result
